user_query:
I need to prepare follow-up reminders and meeting notes for our upcoming layout review, with special attention to my dad ("Parent (Pat)"), whose aesthetic feedback I rely on heavily. Please work entirely from the local inputs below, and write all outputs under out/.

Inputs to use:
- input/reminder_config.yaml: rules for generating reminders
- input/issue_schedule.csv: per-spread schedule dates for trigger events
- input/layout_feedback.jsonl: recent feedback items from parent and team, including status

Deliverables:
1) out/reminders.csv (structured reminders)
2) out/meeting_notes.md (meeting notes with action items and prioritized parent follow-up)
3) out/consistency_report.json (cross-checks and counts)

Requirements:
- Inspect the configuration in input/reminder_config.yaml to drive all reminder creation. For each rule, create a reminder for every spread in input/issue_schedule.csv that has a non-empty date for that rule’s trigger. Compute due_date = trigger_date + offset_days. If due_date falls on Saturday or Sunday, shift it to the next Monday and record this in the consistency report.
- Write out/reminders.csv with these columns: spread_id, article_title, reminder_name, assignee, channel, source_event, trigger_date, due_date. Only include reminders that were actually created (i.e., the trigger date exists in the schedule).
- Determine meeting_date as one day after the latest non-empty date found across all event columns in input/issue_schedule.csv. Use this derived meeting_date in meeting notes (do not use today’s date).
- Prepare out/meeting_notes.md with three sections in this order:
  a) "Parent priorities": For each spread that has at least one open feedback from from == "Parent (Pat)" in input/layout_feedback.jsonl, include a bullet with [PARENT], spread_id, article_title, and the most recent such open feedback summary (by date), and set the item due date to meeting_date.
  b) "Action Items (rule-based reminders)": Summarize all reminders from out/reminders.csv in human-readable bullets, sorted by due_date then spread_id. Each bullet must include spread_id, article_title, reminder_name, assignee, channel, source_event, due_date.
  c) "Open Feedback (latest per spread)": For each spread that has any open feedback (status == "open") in input/layout_feedback.jsonl, include the single most recent open item (by date) showing spread_id, article_title, from, date, and summary. If the latest open item is from "Parent (Pat)", prepend [PARENT].
- Write out/consistency_report.json with at least these fields:
  {
    "meeting_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "total_reminders": <int>,
    "skipped_missing_event_dates": { "<rule_name>": ["<spread_id>", ...], ... },
    "due_dates_adjusted_to_weekday": [
      {"spread_id": "...", "reminder_name": "...", "original_due_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "adjusted_due_date": "YYYY-MM-DD"}
    ],
    "unknown_spread_ids_in_feedback": ["..."]
  }
  - skipped_missing_event_dates: spreads that lacked the trigger date for each rule.
  - due_dates_adjusted_to_weekday: entries only if any due_date was moved from a weekend.
  - unknown_spread_ids_in_feedback: any spread_ids present in input/layout_feedback.jsonl that are not present in input/issue_schedule.csv.

Notes:
- Treat the YAML rules as the source of truth for creating reminders (this makes inspecting the configuration essential). All outputs must be derived from the provided inputs; do not assume any additional spreads or dates.
- Keep outputs deterministic: rely only on the input files and the rules above.

Output paths:
- Reminders CSV: out/reminders.csv
- Meeting notes: out/meeting_notes.md
- Consistency report: out/consistency_report.json All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
